page: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 8 vCPUs, derived from 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz and is equipped with 48 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and a large 480 MB L3 cache. The instance includes 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data highlights strong single-core and text processing capabilities. The instance achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2180.0 and a text processing score of 2984.0, both placing it in the top tier (top 10%). Single-threaded compression and decompression performance are also strong, with gzip compression reaching 34.38 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression reaching 14.47 GB/sec. For lightweight machine learning tasks, the instance demonstrates strong text generation performance using a small 135M parameter LLM model, achieving 288.8 tokens/sec. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations generally fall within the average range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8i-flex.2xlarge offers a balanced resource profile suitable for general-purpose workloads without the premium cost of specialized storage or GPU-accelerated hardware. However, the lack of local NVMe storage and a relatively low baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps represent clear tradeoffs for I/O-intensive applications. This instance is best suited for workloads such as web serving, software compilation, text processing, and light machine learning inference where single-core performance is critical but massive parallel scaling or local high-speed storage is not required.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an 8-vCPU Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance provides dedicated CPU allocation with a 480 MB L3 cache but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show exceptional single-core capabilities, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores ranking in the top 10% tier. Additionally, single-threaded gzip compression and small-model LLM text generation perform strongly. While multi-core operations and memory bandwidth remain in the average range, the instance offers a balanced, cost-efficient profile for general-purpose workloads. It is highly suited for applications such as web serving, text processing, software compilation, and lightweight machine learning inference where high single-thread performance is required without the need for local NVMe storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.2xlarge is an 8-vCPU general-purpose instance with an Intel Xeon CPU, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier single-core performance, ideal for web serving and text processing.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 8 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier single-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring high-speed DDR5 memory and exceptional single-core processing performance for diverse enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-vCPU Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier single-core and text processing benchmark performance
Features a large 480 MB L3 cache and advanced vector/matrix extensions
Lacks local storage and GPU, utilizing a 2 Gbps baseline network
Best suited for web serving, software compilation, and light LLM inference
